positioning.one_liner: Graph-assisted repurposing hypothesis exploration with clinician review guardrails.
jobs_to_be_done: Map mechanisms, target-disease associations, clinical signals, and safety transfer.
Rank sparse-evidence disease hypotheses using transparent disease similarity support.
Separate indication signals from contraindication and safety-risk signals.
Compare method-family signals instead of overfitting product claims to one paper.
Expose false-positive risk and false-negative gap checks before validation claims.
Generate transparent graph paths behind candidate links.
Route explanation paths, external usage signals, and validation gaps for scientific review.

guardrails: Do not present candidates as recommended therapy.
Do not treat co-mention, relation extraction, or graph path scoring as causal proof.
Do not claim TxGNN-level or foundation-model performance without an actual validated model integration and benchmark.
Do not turn a single research article into a product claim; convert it into a governed method signal.
Treat external usage signals as confounded review inputs, not causal or validation evidence.
Every candidate must remain hypothesis-only until reviewed and validated.
